description: secretx-k8s/src/lib.rs:185-198. With ?key=, when PATCH returns 404 (Secret absent), code falls through to POST create at line 242. Two writers racing both observe 404, both POST create; the second receives 409 AlreadyExists which map_kube_error routes to SecretError::Backend (permanent). Outcome: caller sees a confusing 'backend k8s error: ...AlreadyExists' on what should succeed (the Secret now exists with the other writer's value) or be retryable. Trigger: any deployment with >1 replica writing the same with-key URI. Fix options: (a) on AlreadyExists, retry the PATCH; (b) detect AlreadyExists in map_kube_error and translate to Unavailable so caller retries; (c) use SSA on the with-key path too with a per-key field manager so create-or-update is atomic.
title: PATCH-404-then-POST race produces AlreadyExists Backend error on concurrent writers
